message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Importance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Response to SARS-CoV-2 vaccines in kidney transplant recipients (KTR) is severely reduced. Heterologous 3
                    <jats:sup>rd</jats:sup>
                    vaccination combining mRNA and vector vaccines did not increase seroconversion at four weeks after vaccination but evolution of antibody levels beyond the first month remain unknown.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    To assess changes in antibody response following a 3
                    <jats:sup>rd</jats:sup>
                    vaccination with mRNA or vector vaccine in KTR from month one to month three after vaccination.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Design, Setting and Participants</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Three-month follow-up (pre-specified secondary endpoint) of a single-center, single-blinded, 1:1 randomized, controlled trial on 3
                    <jats:sup>rd</jats:sup>
                    vaccination against SARS-CoV-2 in 201 KTR who did not develop SARS-CoV-2 spike protein antibodies following two doses of an mRNA vaccine.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Intervention(s)</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    mRNA (BNT162b2 or mRNA-1273) or vector (Ad26COVS1) as 3
                    <jats:sup>rd</jats:sup>
                    SARS-CoV-2 vaccine
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Main Outcomes and Measures</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Main outcome was seroconversion at the second follow-up between 60-120 days after the 3
                    <jats:sup>rd</jats:sup>
                    vaccination. Subsequently, higher cut-off levels associated with neutralizing capacity and protective immunity were applied (i.e. >15, >100, >141 and >264 BAU/mL). In addition, trajectories of antibody levels from month one to month three were analyzed. Finally, SARS-CoV-2 specific CD4 and CD8 T-cells at four weeks were compared among the 18 top responders in both groups.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A total of 169 patients were available for the three-month follow-up. Overall, seroconversion at three months was similar between both groups (45% versus 50% for mRNA and vector group, respectively; OR=1.24, 95%CI=[0.65, 2.37], p=0.539). However, when applying higher cut-off levels, a significantly larger number of individual in the vector group reached antibody levels > 141 and > 264 BAU/mL at the three-month follow-up (141 BAU/mL: 4% vs. 15% OR=4.96, 95%CI=[1.29, 28.21], p=0.009 and 264 BAU/mL: 1% vs. 10% OR=8.75, 95%CI=[1.13, 396.17], p=0.018 for mRNA vs. vector vaccine group, respectively). In line, antibody levels in seroconverted patients further increased from month one to month three in the vector group while remaining unchanged in the mRNA group (median increase: mRNA= 1.35 U/mL and vector = 27.6 U/mL, p = 0.004). Of particular note, there was no difference in the CD4 and CD8 T-cell response between the mRNA and vector vaccine group at month one.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions and Relevance</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Despite a similar overall seroconversion rate at three months following 3
                    <jats:sup>rd</jats:sup>
                    vaccination in KTR, a heterologous 3rd booster vaccination with Ad26COVS1 resulted in significantly higher antibody levels in responders.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Trial Registration</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>EurdraCT: 2021-002927-39</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Three-month follow-up of heterologous vs homologous third vaccination in kidney transplant recipients

reference.unstructured: Grupper A , Rabinowich L , Schwartz D , et al. Reduced humoral response to mRNA SARS-CoV-2 BNT162b2 vaccine in kidney transplant recipients without prior exposure to the virus. Am J Transplant. 2021.

Reindl-Schwaighofer R , Heinzel A , Mayrdorfer M , et al. Comparison of SARS-CoV-2 Antibody Response 4 Weeks After Homologous vs Heterologous Third Vaccine Dose in Kidney Transplant Recipients: A Randomized Clinical Trial. JAMA Intern Med. 2021.

reference.article-title: Cellular immunity predominates over humoral immunity after homologous and heterologous mRNA and vector-based COVID-19 vaccine regimens in solid organ transplant recipients

reference.unstructured: Bonelli M , Mrak D , Tobudic S , et al. Additional heterologous versus homologous booster vaccination in immunosuppressed patients without SARS-CoV-2 antibody seroconversion after primary mRNA vaccination: a randomised controlled trial. Ann Rheum Dis. 2022.

reference.article-title: The Omicron variant is highly resistant against antibody-mediated neutralization: Implications for control of the COVID-19 pandemic
